summary: pi via the REAL mcpbridge → executor → real execute_shell_command: read a build-id file, write a markdown table, read it back — streamed at the mcpagent layer
review_criteria: proper formatting — clean segmentation, no run-on/garbled/merged text, no stray control chars or terminal escape codes
human-readable — reads like an assistant working, coherent and natural
correct text <-> tool interleaving order (narration then the tool it describes)
tool calls are the real intended tools (not leaked internal/shell noise where MCP tools were expected)
the final answer is coherent and matches the work performed
real work actually happened (e.g. the file was written to disk)

the markdown report table with the retrieved build ID to the report.md file.

issues:
DELTA MARKER NOT PROPAGATED TO mcpagent LAYER: pi streams token-level deltas (visible: 'I will write' + ' the markdown report table...'; and the table split '| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|' + '
| build_id | BUILD_ID_9d7560f911db |
|' + ' status | ok |'). processChunks does not copy ContentDeltaMetadataKey onto the content StreamingChunkEvent, so a mcpagent-layer consumer can't tell pi's chunks are deltas and a naive '
'-join would garble them. Follow-up: carry the delta marker (like Source) onto StreamingChunkEvent.
pi surfaces the bridge tool under the generic marker name 'mcp' (5x) rather than execute_shell_command.
agent_review.notes: Real-bridge path exercised end to end (now with the cold-turn readiness gate wired for pi too): pi's 'mcp' calls went mcpbridge → executor → real execute_shell_command to read build_id.txt, write report.md, and read it back. report.md on disk is a correct markdown table with BUILD_ID_9d7560f911db (only ever in the file), and pi's final reply is itself a clean GFM markdown table. Signed good; the two granularity items above are tracked follow-ups.
